articles.title: Riyadh property market gains investor attention amid Vision 2030
articles.summary: Riyadh's real estate market is attracting increased international investor interest as Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 economic diversification programme drives infrastructure spending and corporate relocations to the capital city. The market, historically dominated by domestic buyers, is transitioning towards an international investment destination as multinational companies establish regional headquarters in the city and foreign ownership routes expand. articles.title: Saudi Arabia property market gains traction with investors
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's property market is attracting increased attention from regional and international investors as the country progresses through economic reforms tied to its Vision 2030 programme. The search for investment opportunities in the Kingdom has intensified as capital markets deepen and infrastructure spending continues. The post Saudi Arabia property market gains traction with investors appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: HOUSE PRICE ANALYSIS BY COUNTRY: Scotland remains most affordable

articles.title: Bridging lender completes £210k auction finance in 17 days
articles.summary: Avamore Capital has completed a £210,000 bridging finance deal for an auction property purchase within 17 working days from acceptance of Heads of Terms. The transaction was finalised four working days after the case transferred to the underwriting team. The post Bridging lender completes £210k auction finance in 17 days appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia’s off-plan market attracts foreign investors
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia's off-plan property market is drawing increased international attention as the Kingdom opens designated ownership zones to foreign buyers and accelerates infrastructure delivery under its Vision 2030 programme. The shift marks a significant change for what has historically been one of the world's largest domestic property markets. The post Saudi Arabia’s off-plan market attracts foreign investors appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Properties near marathon routes listed across England
articles.summary: Five residential properties located along marathon and half-marathon routes across England have been listed for sale, with prices ranging from £350,000 to £1.6 million. The listings span from Brighton to Yorkshire, including properties in London's Dalston and Woolwich areas. The post Properties near marathon routes listed across England appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Landlord property sales decline 45% as rental exodus slows
articles.summary: The proportion of landlords selling former rental properties has fallen by almost half over the past year, according to new data from TwentyCi. The share of homes coming to market that were previously rented dropped from 22.5% in Q1 2025 to 12.4% in Q1 2026, representing a year-on-year reduction of 45%. The post Landlord property sales decline 45% as rental exodus slows appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Investor group challenges leasehold reforms over £30bn losses
articles.summary: A coalition of investors and freeholders has launched a campaign to challenge leasehold reforms, claiming losses could exceed £30 billion. The group, Justice for Property Rights, is seeking legal advice on bringing a collective action before the European Court of Human Rights. The post Investor group challenges leasehold reforms over £30bn losses appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Property transactions take over 17 weeks to exchange
articles.summary: Property transactions in the UK are taking significantly longer to complete, with 43% of housing sales now requiring more than 17 weeks to reach exchange. The trade body described the figure as the highest level recorded, highlighting ongoing inefficiencies in the transaction process. The post Property transactions take over 17 weeks to exchange appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: UK property transactions down 7.6% year-on-year in 2026
articles.summary: UK property sales have declined 7.6% year-to-date compared to 2025, with 342,000 homes sold subject to contract by mid-April 2026. The figures show transactions remain below last year's levels but continue to outperform 2024 by 5.6% and sit 12.9% above pre-Covid norms. The post UK property transactions down 7.6% year-on-year in 2026 appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Hinckley HMO restrictions approved despite divided response
articles.summary: Hinckley and Bosworth Borough Council has approved Article 4 Direction restrictions on HMOs in several streets, despite a divided consultation response. The policy received 65% support overall but revealed significant splits between residents inside and outside affected areas, with concerns ranging from parking congestion to immigration-related issues. The post Hinckley HMO restrictions approved despite divided response appeared first on PropertyWire.
